package_status.lmcache: installed 0.4.6; top-level import passes, CUDA c_ops fails due to libcudart.so.13

blocker.summary: LMCache/vLLM GPU wheels require a CUDA/PyTorch/vLLM/LMCache stack newer than this host currently exposes. Current driver reports CUDA 12.0; LMCache installation docs target CUDA 12.1+ and current wheels target newer CUDA stacks.
details: No nvcc is installed, so local CUDA extension compilation is not a viable fallback.
Host GCC is 7.5.0, also risky for modern vLLM/LMCache source builds.
Docker/Podman is unavailable, so official container fallback cannot be used on this host without admin setup.
GPU hardware compute capability 7.5 is sufficient for small-model smoke, but software stack is not ready.

next_actions: Upgrade NVIDIA driver to a version compatible with the target CUDA wheel, preferably CUDA 12.9/13.0 per current LMCache docs, or at least CUDA 12.1+ for older compatible stacks.
Alternatively run on a server/container that already has compatible driver and CUDA runtime.
After driver/container is ready, install torch, transformers, vllm, and lmcache in the h3-lmcache environment and rerun validation commands.
Reserve one idle RTX 2080 Ti for smoke tests.

evidence: vllm 0.6.6.post1 wheel is available for cp38-abi3 and metadata pins torch==2.5.1, torchvision==0.20.1, xformers==0.0.28.post3.
lmcache 0.3.15 wheel is available for cp312.
lmcache 0.3.15 still depends on cupy-cuda12x and cufile-python, so current driver may still be insufficient.
downgrade_candidate.recommended_next_step: Try the cu118 torch/vllm/lmcache downgrade install only if package download is acceptable; validate import and torch.cuda.is_available immediately after.

validation.lmcache_top_level_import: passed with LD_LIBRARY_PATH; CUDA c_ops backend falls back after libcudart.so.13 failure

remaining_blockers: LMCache 0.4.6 requires cupy-cuda13x / CUDA 13 runtime for its CUDA c_ops, but this host has torch CUDA 12.4 runtime and nvidia-smi reports CUDA 12.0.
Installing cupy-cuda13x did not expose libcudart.so.13 to LMCache on this host and created dependency conflicts, so it was removed.
pip check still reports LMCache dependencies missing: cupy-cuda13x, opentelemetry-exporter-prometheus; these are intentionally omitted to keep vLLM 0.8.5 importable.
